# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Istio ServiceEntry and VirtualService configurations for external service access. Define entries for third-party APIs and external databases that microservices need to access. Configure DNS resolution and traffic routing to external hosts. This is for our service mesh egress configuration.
# Category: istio_egress_unrestricted

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: ServiceEntry
metadata: 
 name: microservices-access   # unique identifier of the entry point (mandatory)      
 namespace: default           # optional, specify a particular Namespace to put resource into        
 spec:   
     endpoints:                # list of possible destinations for this service Entry.      Required unless `selectors` is provided                 
     - address: <external-address>  
 ports:                       # set up the protocol and port information  Optional, but recommended                  
       - name: http               # Name to use in traffic rules (mandatory)                     defaults a specific rule if not specified.          Port number must be unique within all Rules                  Allowed values are HTTP/TCP based protocols                    Protocols other than HTTP2 may require 'http' port for TCP connections                
     - name: https              # Another example field                   Defaults to the first matching `port`  If no specific rule is provided, defaults a Rule with HTTPS if not specified.      Allowed values are any combination of above protocols (i.e., http/https) and others like 'h2', wss  
     selectors:                # A list selector fields that you can use to identify the service entry from multiple sources                   Optional, but recommended for more fine-grained control    Example 1 `app` : demo app - this is a string field example of where it could come in handy.                    Required if provided when creating or updating ServiceEntries  
     - matchLabels:            # A list selector labels fields that you can use to identify the service entry from multiple sources      Optional, but recommended for more fine-grained control Example 2 `app` : demo app             Similar requirement as above                    Required if provided when creating or updating ServiceEntries
